propositions: Releases must be initiated from the protected main branch via workflow_dispatch with version-lockstep assertion, eliminating the tag-triggered publish path so a hand-pushed v* tag cannot publish.

A v1.0 binary must read a committed store compatibility record and fail closed on present-but-incompatible or corrupt records across all store-mutation and publish paths, while treating an absent record as legacy version 0 (compatible) to preserve upgrade-in-place and rollback safety.
The build-once flow must test the exact wheel and sdist that get published (hash-verified), and the shipped wheel must carry a correct non-null source commit rather than COMMIT=None.
Every release-dependent executable must be pinned to an immutable reference (full action SHAs, hash-locked build env, digest-verified MCP publisher) with credentials not persisted in checkouts.

Removed env vars and TOML keys affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, or lifecycle policy must fail loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit, while unknown keys retain forward-compatibility behaviour.
